title: Tuning the Electrochemical Stability Window of Li3YCl6 by Fluorine Doping to Enable 4.8 V Solid-State Battery
authors: Lily Mandal, Susmita Bera, Ripan K. Biswas and Abhik Banerjee
abstract: Lithium batteries with enhanced energy density and safety require solid electrolytes having stability with High voltage cathodes and Li metal. Here, halide solid electrolyte Li3YCl6 is doped with Fluorine to simultaneously increase the cathodic and anodic stability. The anodic and cathodic stability was confirmed by cyclic voltammetry, impedance spectroscopy and operando pressure measurement. Further, operando X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) during Li plating on Li3YCl5.9F0.1 was done to study effect of Fluorine on reaction kinetics driving the evolution of interphase with Li metal and …

title: Trifluoroacetic Anhydride: A Dual-Function Electrolyte Additive for Cycle-Life Extension and Flame Retardancy in Li-Metal Batteries
authors: Lintong Zhou, Chengliang Wang and Jingjiang Sun
abstract: Lithium batteries are pivotal for modern energy storage, yet their performance and safety are critically influenced by electrolyte stability. This study systematically investigates three structurally similar fluorinated anhydrides, difluoroacetic anhydride (DFAA), trifluoroacetic anhydride (TFAA), and pentafluoropropionic anhydride (PFPA), as multifunctional electrolyte additives for lithium metal batteries. Electrochemical evaluations reveal that the addition of 1 wt% TFAA significantly enhances the cycling stability of both LiFePO4 (LFP) and LiNi0.6Co0.2Mn0.2O2 (NCM622) cathodes. After 400 c…

title: Ti-Oxide Doping Alleviated Defects and Introduced Ti–O–Si Bonds to Enhance the Cycling Stability of the Silicon‑Carbon Anode
authors: Songlin Yu, Pengtao Wang, Jiangtao Liang, Song Yang, Junhao Liu and Xuzhong Gong
abstract: Silicon-based anode materials have low electrical conductivity, volume expansion and the easy repeated formation of solid electrolyte interface layers, which seriously impede their commercialization. Introducing Ti–O–Si bonds into silicon-based materials has been proven to be an effective way to alleviate silicon defects. Here, we adopted a one-step mechanical force strategy to synthesize Ti–O–Si bonds, and high-energy ball milling was employed to promote the exfoliation of the lamellar structure of natural graphite spherical tailings and restructure it. During the process, nano-Si and Ti4O7 w…

title: Unlocking hidden sodium
abstract: Published online: 12 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02060-3 Fe-based polyanionic cathodes are promising for large-scale Na-ion batteries but are limited by incomplete Na utilization. Now, research shows that tuning the local Na coordination via V substitution in phosphate-based cathode allows additional Na sites to participate, enabling near-complete Na utilization, enhancing energy density and cycling stability.

title: Harmonized sodium coordination engineering for high-energy phosphate cathodes
abstract: Published online: 12 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02059-w Fe-based polyanionic cathodes are promising for Na-ion batteries but are limited by inactive Na sites and irreversible Na loss. Here the authors employ targeted V3+ substitution to tune the Na+ coordination environment, activate inert sites and stabilize high-voltage redox for high-performance Na-ion batteries.
